mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the past month lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, which supports higher S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if rate cuts are not followed by positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: If monthly declines in FEDFUNDS do not coincide with above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the triggered win rate is below 50% over any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over three months, labor stress is improving sufficiently to raise expectations for future policy support and cyclical relief, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. If unemployment rises without market support, the signal fails.
failure_condition: Reject if instances of a >0.3pp 3-month rise in UNRATE are followed by negative or flat 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the effect disappears in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized pickup in RSAFS and GDPC1 indicates resilient final demand and revenue support for large-cap equities, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when sales growth is not matched by output growth.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint rise in RSAFS and GDPC1 does not improve 60d SPX returns versus baseline, or if either series rising alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO combined with weaker HOUST signals an industrial and housing slowdown that compresses earnings expectations and weighs on cyclicals, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when both indicators deteriorate together.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous monthly declines in INDPRO and HOUST are not followed by below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above its recent trend, real discount-rate pressure rises and equity multiples compress, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The pattern should be strongest immediately after an upside inflation surprise.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month moving trend
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-trend crossings are not followed by below-zero 20d SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal loses sign consistency across two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in PAYEMS alongside stable or lower UNRATE signals improving household income and demand resilience, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through a stronger earnings outlook. The effect fails if job growth is offset by rising unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS gains combined with non-rising UNRATE do not produce positive 5d SPX returns, or if PAYEMS alone explains the signal better than the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a rise in FEDFUNDS above 4.5% increases the drag from financing costs and valuation compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is inactive when policy rates remain near zero.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not precede below-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the sign reverses during sustained tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising RSAFS together with improving INDPRO and falling UNRATE indicates a broad demand rebound that should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if sales growth occurs without labor improvement or industrial recovery.
failure_condition: Reject if the three-way recovery pattern does not predict positive 60d SPX returns, or if equivalent returns are observed when only one of the three series improves.
